prompt: Share an untranslatable word or idiom from any language you know about — something that doesn't have a clean English equivalent — and explain what it captures.
response: In Japanese, there is an idiom called "Kizudarumademo Gaman Yasashii" which translates to "Even if it hurts, I'll keep going because it's gentle." This idiom captures the idea of perseverance and resilience in the face of adversity. It suggests that even when something is painful or difficult, one should continue because there is an underlying kindness or gentleness that will guide them through it. This idiom is often used to encourage people to keep going despite their struggles, reminding them that there is always a reason to keep moving forward.
